common.privacy_note: Everything runs in your browser. Nothing is stored or transmitted until you choose to register.
common.disclaimer: This is an educational screening tool, not a diagnostic instrument. It does not replace professional medical evaluation.

landing.title: You know this feeling
landing.hook: It's 3am. You're staring at the ceiling again. The alarm is set for 6:30 and your mind is already calculating — three and a half hours, if you fall asleep right now. But you won't fall asleep right now. You know that. Your body is exhausted but something in you won't let go.
landing.hook_2: Or maybe it's the fog at 2pm that no amount of coffee cuts through. The short temper with someone you love over something trivial. The alarm that goes off when it feels like you just closed your eyes. You know something is wrong with your sleep. You just don't know what, or where to start.
landing.section_body: Deep sleep is your body's maintenance window. Growth hormone release, tissue repair, immune recalibration, cardiovascular recovery, metabolic regulation. This isn't optional recovery — it's work your body literally cannot do while you're awake. Chronic sleep deprivation produces measurable increases in inflammatory markers, insulin resistance, and cortisol dysregulation. The damage accumulates silently and compounds.
landing.section_emotional: The amygdala becomes roughly 60% more reactive after sleep deprivation while the prefrontal cortex — the part that keeps your reactions proportionate — becomes less active. This isn't a metaphor; it's measurable on brain imaging. Irritability that feels disproportionate, emotional reactions you can't explain, reduced empathy, difficulty navigating conflict. Many people treating anxiety or mood instability are actually treating a sleep problem they haven't identified.
landing.section_cognitive: Working memory, executive function, creative problem-solving, learning consolidation — all degrade with poor sleep. After 17-19 hours awake, cognitive impairment is equivalent to a blood alcohol level of 0.05%. The critical detail: unlike alcohol, sleep deprivation doesn't come with self-awareness of impairment. You make worse decisions and don't notice.
landing.section_financial: Sleep-deprived professionals show measurable productivity losses, worse negotiation outcomes, poorer strategic decisions. For anyone whose livelihood depends on thinking clearly, every foggy morning is diminished earning capacity.
landing.what_this_does: A structured self-assessment built on validated clinical screening instruments. It examines six dimensions of your life that affect sleep: your environment, your biology, your health history, your personal habits, your emotional state, and your sense of safety. It identifies patterns, explains the mechanisms, and gives you a prioritized action plan for your specific situation.

step1.bedtime_work: What time do you usually get into bed on a work night?
step1.bedtime_free: What about nights when you have no obligations the next morning?
step1.waketime_work: What time do you usually try to wake up on work days?
step1.sleep_onset: Once you're in bed and trying to sleep, roughly how long does it take before you actually fall asleep?
step1.night_wakings: How many times do you typically wake up during the night?
step1.night_wakings.0: I don't wake up during the night
step1.sleep_hours: How many hours of actual sleep do you think you're getting on a typical night?
step1.main_struggle: Which of these best describes your main struggle with sleep?
step1.main_struggle.cant_fall_asleep: I can't fall asleep. I lie there awake.
step1.main_struggle.wake_during_night: I fall asleep fine but keep waking up during the night.
step1.main_struggle.wake_too_early: I wake up way too early and can't get back to sleep.
step1.main_struggle.unrestorative: I sleep through the night but wake up feeling like I didn't sleep at all.
step1.main_struggle.excessive_sleepiness: I'm unbearably sleepy during the day no matter how much I sleep.
step1.main_struggle.parasomnias: Strange things happen while I sleep — movements, walking, talking, vivid acting-out of dreams.
step1.main_struggle.irregular_schedule: My sleep schedule is all over the place. I can't get into a rhythm.
step1.main_struggle.multiple: Several of these apply to me.
step1.duration: How long has this been going on?
step1.duration.weeks: Just the last couple of weeks
step1.duration.always: As long as I can remember
step1.frequency: How many nights per week does this happen?
step1.frequency.couple_nights: A couple of nights a week
step1.isi: How your sleep problem affects you
step1.isi.intro: These next questions help us gauge the severity of what you're experiencing. Answer based on the last two weeks.
step1.isi.falling_asleep: How difficult has it been to fall asleep recently?
step1.isi.staying_asleep: How much trouble have you had staying asleep?
step1.isi.waking_early: Do you have a problem waking up too early?
step1.isi.satisfaction: How satisfied or dissatisfied are you with your current sleep?
step1.isi.noticeable: How noticeable to others do you think your sleep problem is, in terms of affecting your quality of life?
step1.isi.worried: How worried or distressed are you about your sleep right now?
step1.isi.interfere: How much does your sleep problem interfere with your daily functioning — energy, relationships, mood, concentration, memory, ability to work?

step2.bedroom_darkness.question: When you're lying in bed at night with the lights off, how dark is the room?
step2.bedroom_darkness.complete_darkness: It's completely dark — I can't see my hand in front of my face
step2.bedroom_darkness.outlines_shapes: I can see outlines and shapes but it's quite dark
step2.bedroom_darkness.noticeable_light: There's noticeable light from streetlights, devices, or gaps in curtains
step2.bedroom_darkness.fairly_lit: The room stays fairly lit — screens, hallway light, or no curtains
step2.bedroom_sound.question: What's the sound environment like in your bedroom at night?
step2.bedroom_sound.occasional: Occasional sounds but nothing that regularly wakes me
step2.bedroom_sound.regular_noise: Regular noise from traffic, neighbors, or household
step2.bedroom_sound.partner_snores: My partner snores or makes noise
step2.bedroom_sound.unpredictable: Unpredictable noises that startle me awake
step2.bedroom_sound.sound_machine: I use a sound machine or fan for background noise
step2.bedroom_temp.question: How would you describe the temperature in your bedroom at night?
step2.bedroom_temp.comfortable: Comfortable — I don't think about it
step2.bedroom_temp.changes: It changes through the night — I wake up hot or cold
step2.bedroom_comfort.question: How comfortable is your mattress and bedding?
step2.bedroom_comfort.comfortable: Very comfortable — I look forward to getting into bed
step2.bedroom_comfort.stiff_sore: I wake up stiff or sore
step2.bedroom_comfort.uncomfortable: It's uncomfortable but I'm used to it
step2.bedroom_comfort.old_mattress: My mattress is old and needs replacing
step2.bedroom_air.question: How is the air quality in your bedroom?
step2.bedroom_air.fresh: Fresh — I keep a window open or have good ventilation
step2.bedroom_air.congested: I often wake up congested or with a dry throat
step2.bed_sharing.question: Do you share your bed with anyone?
step2.bed_sharing.partner_no_issue: With a partner — they don't disrupt my sleep
step2.bed_sharing.partner_disrupts: With a partner — their movements, snoring, or schedule disrupts me
step2.pets.question: Do pets sleep in your bedroom?
step2.pets.pets_no_issue: Yes, but they don't disturb my sleep
step2.pets.pets_wake_me: Yes, and they sometimes wake me up
step2.evening_activities.question: What do you typically do in the hour before bed? (Select all that apply)
step2.evening_content.question: What kind of content do you typically consume in the evening?
step2.evening_content.engaging: Engaging shows, games, or deep reading
step2.evening_content.stressful: Content that often leaves me activated or upset
step2.evening_content.no_screens: I mostly avoid screens in the evening
step2.evening_lighting.question: What's the lighting like in your home in the evening?
step2.evening_lighting.bright_overhead: Bright overhead lights stay on until bedtime
step2.evening_lighting.dimmed: I dim lights or switch to lamps in the evening
step2.evening_lighting.dark_screens_only: It's mostly dark except for screens
step2.bed_activities.question: What do you use your bed for besides sleep?
step2.bed_activities.watch_in_bed: I watch shows or scroll in bed
step2.bed_activities.multiple: Multiple activities — my bed is my main living space
step2.environment_noise.question: How noisy is your living environment overall?
step2.environment_noise.moderate: Moderate — some traffic or neighbor noise
step2.shift_work.question: Do you do shift work or have irregular work hours?
step2.shift_work.no: No — I work roughly the same hours each day

step3.physical_profile.neck_size: How would you describe your neck size?
step3.physical_profile.neck_size.large: Large — I need larger collar sizes
step3.physical_profile.weight_distribution: Where do you tend to carry extra weight?
step3.breathing.snoring: Has anyone told you that you snore?
step3.breathing.snoring.no: No, or I've been told I don't snore
step3.breathing.witnessed_apnea: Has anyone noticed you stop breathing during sleep?
step3.breathing.witnessed_apnea.yes: Yes — someone has told me this
step3.breathing.choking: Do you ever wake up gasping or choking?
step3.breathing.morning_symptoms: Do you wake up with any of these? (Select all that apply)
step3.breathing.mouth_breathing: Do you breathe through your mouth during sleep?
step3.breathing.mouth_breathing.yes: Yes — I'm a mouth breather at night
step3.breathing.mouth_breathing.no: No — I breathe through my nose
step3.breathing.teeth_grinding: Do you grind your teeth at night?
step3.breathing.teeth_grinding.yes: Yes — confirmed by a dentist or partner
step3.breathing.teeth_grinding.suspected: I suspect I do — jaw pain or worn teeth
step3.movement: What your body does while you sleep
step3.movement.restless_legs: Do you get an uncomfortable urge to move your legs when lying down?
step3.movement.restless_legs.yes: Yes — it's a strong, uncomfortable sensation
step3.movement.restless_relief: Does moving your legs relieve the sensation?
step3.movement.leg_jerking: Has anyone told you your legs jerk or kick during sleep?
step3.movement.dream_acting: Do you physically act out your dreams — punching, kicking, falling out of bed?
step3.movement.sleepwalking: Do you sleepwalk, sleep-talk, or do complex things while asleep?
step3.movement.sleep_paralysis: Do you ever wake up unable to move your body?
step3.movement.sleep_paralysis.yes: Yes — it's terrifying and happens regularly
step3.movement.hypnagogic: Do you see or hear things while falling asleep or waking up that aren't there?
step3.movement.hypnagogic.yes: Yes — vivid images, sounds, or presences
step3.internal_clock.natural_sleep_time: If you had no obligations, what time would you naturally fall asleep?
step3.internal_clock.natural_wake_time: And what time would you naturally wake up?
step3.internal_clock.peak_alertness: When do you feel most alert and productive?
step3.internal_clock.adaptability: How easily do you adjust to schedule changes?
step3.internal_clock.adaptability.moderate: It takes me a few days
step3.internal_clock.adaptability.difficult: I struggle significantly with any shift
step3.internal_clock.vacation_sleep: How do you sleep on vacation or when obligations are removed?
step3.internal_clock.vacation_sleep.much_bette: Much better — I sleep like a different person
step3.internal_clock.vacation_sleep.worse: Actually worse — unfamiliar environments bother me
step3.internal_clock.schedule_gap: How different is your sleep schedule on work days vs. free days?
step3.hormonal.cortisol_surge: Do you wake up between 2-4am feeling suddenly alert or anxious?
step3.hormonal.cortisol_surge.yes: Yes — this is a regular pattern
step3.hormonal.early_waking_pattern: When you wake too early, do you feel alert or groggy?
step3.hormonal.early_waking_pattern.yes_alert: Alert — my mind switches on immediately
step3.hormonal.early_waking_pattern.yes_groggy: Groggy — I want to sleep but can't
step3.hormonal.post_meal_drowsy: Do you feel extremely drowsy after meals?
step3.hormonal.post_meal_drowsy.yes: Yes — I sometimes need to nap after eating
step3.hormonal.post_meal_drowsy.no: No — meals don't affect my alertness much
step3.hormonal.afternoon_crash: Do you experience a strong afternoon energy crash?
step3.hormonal.afternoon_crash.no: No — my energy stays relatively stable
step3.hormonal_women.cycle_sleep: Does your menstrual cycle affect your sleep?
step3.hormonal_women.cycle_sleep.yes: Yes — noticeably worse at certain times
step3.hormonal_women.cycle_sleep.somewhat: Somewhat — I notice a mild pattern
step3.hormonal_women.perimenopause: Are you experiencing perimenopause or menopause symptoms?
step3.hormonal_women.perimenopause.yes: Yes — hot flashes, night sweats, or cycle changes
step3.hormonal_women.pregnant: Are you pregnant or recently postpartum?
step3.hormonal_men.nocturia: How often do you wake up to urinate at night?
step3.hormonal_men.nocturia.none: I don't wake up for this
step3.hormonal_men.energy_changes: Have you noticed changes in energy, motivation, or body composition?
step3.thyroid_everyone.cold_sluggish: Do you often feel cold, sluggish, or have unexplained weight gain?
step3.thyroid_everyone.warm_wired: Do you feel wired, warm, or have unexplained weight loss?
step3.family.family_sleep_problems: Do immediate family members have sleep problems?
step3.family.family_sleep_problems.yes: Yes — diagnosed or obvious sleep disorders
step3.family.family_sleep_problems.possibly: Possibly — some family members sleep poorly

step4.medication_text: List any medications or supplements you take regularly
step4.medication_text.placeholder: e.g., sertraline 50mg, melatonin 3mg, ibuprofen as needed...
step4.medication_categories: Which categories apply? (Select all that apply)
step4.caffeine_type: What caffeine sources do you consume? (Select all that apply)
step4.caffeine_amount: How many caffeinated drinks per day?
step4.caffeine_timing: When do you have your last caffeinated drink?
step4.alcohol_frequency: How often do you drink alcohol?
step4.alcohol_timing: When do you usually drink relative to bedtime?
step4.alcohol_sleep_pattern: How does alcohol affect your sleep?
step4.alcohol_sleep_pattern.helps_fall_asleep_: Helps me fall asleep but I sleep worse later
step4.nicotine_timing: When do you last use nicotine?
step4.cannabis_for_sleep: Do you use cannabis specifically to help with sleep?
step4.cannabis_for_sleep.yes: Yes — it's my main sleep aid
step4.cannabis_for_sleep.no: No — I use it for other reasons
step4.pain_at_night: Does physical pain interfere with your sleep?
step4.pain_at_night.yes: Yes — pain regularly disrupts my sleep
step4.gerd: Do you experience acid reflux or heartburn at night?
step4.breathing_difficulty: Do you have difficulty breathing when lying down?
step4.breathing_difficulty.yes: Yes — I need to prop myself up
step4.headaches: When do you typically get headaches?
step4.headaches.morning: Morning — I wake up with them
step4.neuropathy: Do you experience tingling, numbness, or burning in your extremities?
step4.fatigue_sensitivity: Do you feel exhausted regardless of how much sleep you get?
step4.fatigue_sensitivity.somewhat: Somewhat — even good nights don't fully recharge me
step4.fatigue_sensitivity.no: No — when I sleep well, I feel refreshed
step4.recent_bloodwork: What did your most recent blood work show? (Select all that apply)
step4.recent_bloodwork.no_recent: I haven't had blood work done recently
step4.what_stopped_working: Is there anything else you've tried? What worked, what didn't?
step4.what_stopped_working.placeholder: Share anything about what you've tried and how it went...

step5.intro: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following? This section uses validated clinical screening questions. Your honest answers help us understand the emotional dimension of your sleep.
step5.little_interest: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
step5.nervous_anxious: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
step5.cant_stop_worrying: Not being able to stop or control worrying
step5.bedtime_dread: Do you dread going to bed?
step5.bedtime_dread.yes: Yes — I avoid it or put it off
step5.bedtime_dread.sometimes: Sometimes — it depends on the night
step5.bedtime_dread.no: No — bedtime is neutral or pleasant
step5.racing_mind: Does your mind race when you lie down?
step5.racing_mind.yes: Yes — it's like a switch flips on
step5.racing_mind.no: No — my mind is usually quiet
step5.clock_watching: Do you check the time during the night?
step5.clock_watching.yes: Yes — and it makes everything worse
step5.clock_watching.no: No — I've stopped looking or removed clocks
step5.trying_hard: Do you feel like you're trying too hard to sleep?
step5.trying_hard.yes: Yes — the harder I try, the less it works
step5.trying_hard.no: No — I don't feel that pressure
step5.easier_elsewhere: Do you sleep better in places other than your own bed?
step5.easier_elsewhere.yes: Yes — hotels, couch, other people's homes
step5.easier_elsewhere.no: No — my bed is where I sleep best
step5.avoidance_scrolling: Do you stay up late scrolling or watching to avoid lying awake?
step5.avoidance_scrolling.yes: Yes — it's easier than facing the dark
step5.main_stressor: What are your main sources of stress right now? (Select all that apply)
step5.coping_pattern: How do you typically cope with stress?
step5.coping_pattern.push_through: Push through — I just keep going
step5.coping_pattern.replay_analyze: Replay and analyze — I go over things in my head
step5.recent_changes: Have there been any significant life changes in the past year that might affect your sleep?
step5.recent_changes.placeholder: e.g., new job, move, breakup, loss, new baby, retirement...
step5.upcoming_anxiety: Is there anything you're dreading or anxious about that keeps you up?
step5.upcoming_anxiety.placeholder: e.g., upcoming deadline, health results, difficult conversation...
step5.trauma.title: Continue to trauma-related questions
step5.trauma.intro: The following questions touch on experiences related to trauma and safety. These factors can significantly affect sleep. You can skip this section entirely if you prefer — it won't affect the rest of your results.
step5.nightmares.yes_recurring: Yes — the same nightmare keeps recurring
step5.nightmares.sometimes: Occasionally, but not a major issue
step5.safety_at_night: Do you feel physically safe when you go to sleep?
step5.startle_response: Do you startle easily at sounds during the night?
step5.startle_response.no: No — sounds don't usually bother me

step6.intro: This final section explores how safe and secure you feel in your life. These factors profoundly affect sleep quality, often more than people realize. Answer honestly — there are no wrong answers.
step6.body_lets_go: When you lie down, does your body actually relax?
step6.body_lets_go.yes: Yes — I feel my body let go
step6.body_lets_go.no: No — I stay rigid or tense
step6.home_safety: Do you feel safe in your home?
step6.home_safety.no: No — I don't feel safe at home
step6.neighborhood_safety.safe: Safe — I don't worry about it
step6.neighborhood_safety.not_safe: Not safe — I'm aware of risks
step6.lock_checking: Do you check locks or windows before bed?
step6.lock_checking.multiple: Multiple times — I need to be sure
step6.lock_checking.no: No — I don't think about it
step6.cohabitant_tension: Is there tension with anyone you live with?
step6.cohabitant_tension.no: No — relationships at home are good
step6.partner_effect: Does your partner's presence affect your ability to sleep?
step6.partner_effect.more_safe: Yes — I feel safer and sleep better with them
step6.partner_effect.more_alert: Yes — I feel more alert or guarded
step6.caregiving_role: Do you have caregiving responsibilities that interrupt your sleep?
step6.caregiving_role.yes: Yes — children, aging parents, or dependents
step6.financial_in_bed: Do financial worries keep you awake at night?
step6.financial_in_bed.yes: Yes — it's a major source of nighttime anxiety
step6.financial_in_bed.no: No — finances aren't a sleep concern
step6.financial_security: How would you describe your overall financial security?
step6.financial_security.secure: Secure — I have savings and stability
step6.last_phone_content: What's typically the last thing you see on your phone before sleep?
step6.last_phone_content.relaxing: Something relaxing — light reading or a podcast
step6.last_phone_content.stressful: Something stressful — work emails, news, arguments
step6.last_phone_content.no_phone: I don't use my phone before bed
step6.evening_news: Do you consume news or social media in the evening?
step6.evening_news.yes_activating: Yes — and it activates or upsets me
step6.evening_news.yes_neutral: Yes — but it doesn't affect me much
step6.evening_news.no: No — I avoid it in the evening
step6.work_email_check: Do you check work email or messages after work hours?
step6.doomscrolling: Do you catch yourself doomscrolling when you should be sleeping?
step6.body_state: When you get into bed at night, how does your body feel?
step6.body_state.tired_wired: Tired but wired — exhausted yet can't settle
step6.body_state.alert_scanning: Alert and scanning — listening for sounds
step6.body_state.numb_disconnected: Numb or disconnected from my body
step6.safety_rituals: What conditions do you need to fall asleep? (Select all that apply)
step6.sleep_away_from_home: How do you sleep away from home?
step6.sleep_away_from_home.better: Better — I feel less pressure or more relaxed
step6.sleep_away_from_home.worse: Worse — unfamiliar places make it harder
